_comment: ALOS has been simplified to '2 days', with additional context moved to additional_information.alos_note. The HBP 2.0 code MP016A is used as the primary key.
clinical_indications: Diagnosis of acute ataxia supported by clinical manifestations such as: Staggered gait (poor balance)
Diagnosis of acute ataxia supported by clinical manifestations such as: Refusal to walk
Diagnosis of acute ataxia supported by clinical manifestations such as: Unsteady and/or clumsy movements
Diagnosis of acute ataxia supported by clinical manifestations such as: Impaired sitting balance
Diagnosis of acute ataxia supported by clinical manifestations such as: Altered mental status
Diagnosis of acute ataxia supported by clinical manifestations such as: Impaired hand coordination
Diagnosis of acute ataxia supported by clinical manifestations such as: Dysarthria or dysphagia
Diagnosis of acute ataxia supported by clinical manifestations such as: Ocular symptoms (visual disturbances/nystagmus)
Diagnosis of acute ataxia supported by clinical manifestations such as: Vomiting
Diagnosis of acute ataxia supported by clinical manifestations such as: Intentional tremor and difficulty in forming speech

preauth.label: Clinical notes showing vitals, examination findings, planned line of treatment and advice for admission

Optional based on etiology and availability: Toxicological testing, blood glucose, metabolic evaluation, Cerebrospinal fluid examination, Viral serology, Urinalysis, Serum Electrolytes, Vitamins, Complete blood count, liver function test

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) with treatment details

ppd_preauth.q: Is the h/o Ataxia with a symptom evolution time of less than 72 hours documented?

common_queries: History of Ataxia with symptom evolution time of 72 hours or more documented
Symptom evolution time for Ataxia not documented
Clinical notes missing or incomplete, lacking detailed history, signs/symptoms, or planned treatment at pre-authorization
Nerve conduction test (ENMG), MRI, or CT scan report missing at pre-authorization
Viral serology report missing at pre-authorization
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) missing or lacking treatment details and patient progress at claim submission
Detailed discharge summary missing or incomplete, lacking follow-up advice at claim submission

clinical_key_pointers: Ataxia refers to a disturbance in the smooth performance of voluntary motor acts and is usually the result of cerebellar dysfunction.
Acute Ataxia is typically defined as ataxia for less than 72 hours, with particular attention to specific diagnoses and relevant diagnostic testing.

Acute ataxia suggests an infectious or postinfectious, endocrinologic, toxic, traumatic, vascular, or psychogenic process, and chronic symptoms suggest a metabolic, neoplastic, or degenerative process.
The most common causes of acute ataxia in childhood are postinfectious acute cerebellar ataxia and drug intoxications.
The key to assessment of acute ataxia in children is a thorough physical examination which may reveal many possible associated findings and shed light on the location of the primary pathology.
additional_information.alos_note: Once diagnosis is established the case can be booked in the relevant package, further stay/admission should be decided based on the level of complications of the disease
medical_management_options: Drug toxicity – remove the underlying inducing agent
Acute labyrinthitis, Meningitis, Cerebellar abscess (small) – Antibiotics
